events.title: Strait of Hormuz Sanctions Policy Change
events.description: The U.S. Treasury's OFAC has revoked General License X and replaced it with General License X1, effective 7 July 2026, impacting sanctioned activities related to the Strait of Hormuz following stalled U.S.–Iran negotiations.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks prompt caution
events.description: Three tanker attacks near the Strait of Hormuz have led to increased caution, with some vessels disabling location signals. Traffic levels remain steady, but the IMO has urged vigilance.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping concerns
events.description: The IMO Secretary-General updated the IMO Council on the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, including evacuation plans, rising insurance costs, and impacts on seafarers. The update highlights ongoing risks and disruptions to vital shipping lanes.

events.title: Maritime piracy risk persists despite decline
events.description: Maritime piracy incidents dropped to their lowest since 1992 in H1 2026, but the International Maritime Bureau warns of ongoing risks, particularly a slight resurgence of Somali piracy in the Gulf of Aden, threatening crew safety and shipping flows.

events.title: Maersk resumes second Suez-Red Sea route with US-flagged vessels
events.description: Maersk announced the restoration of a second shipping route through the Suez Canal and Red Sea, following earlier disruptions. The move involves US-flagged vessels and reflects a cautious return to normal operations in the region.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd revises AL4 North Atlantic service rotation
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd is updating its AL4 service rotation between Northern Europe and North America, effective 16 July 2026. The revised route includes calls at Southampton, Rotterdam, Antwerp, Bremerhaven, Wilhelmshaven, Veracruz, Altamira, and Miami.

events.title: Hormuz Strait War-Risk Insurance Rises
events.description: Shipowners are reducing activity through the Strait of Hormuz as war-risk insurance costs rise, reflecting heightened tensions between the US and Iran and growing concerns over regional security.

events.title: Modified berthing policy for liquid cargo
events.description: A modified berthing policy for liquid cargo has been implemented at Deendayal (Kandla) Port, India, effective from 11:00 hours on July 10, 2026.

events.title: Ukraine strikes 35 vessels in Sea of Azov in 96 hours
events.description: Ukraine reports it has struck 35 vessels in the Sea of Azov over 96 hours, with two additional vessels hit in the Black Sea. The attacks are part of an intensified drone campaign affecting regional maritime operations.

events.title: Mombasa Port Faces Fuel Handling Challenge
events.description: Mombasa Port, which has gained market share due to Red Sea disruptions, now faces a test in handling a tenfold increase in fuel volumes from Rwanda, raising questions about its operational capacity beyond container handling.

events.title: Port Freeport secures Mitsubishi vehicle imports
events.description: Port Freeport has added new vehicle imports and secured a $11 million grant, with Volkswagen and Höegh celebrating milestones. Mitsubishi has also joined the port's growing automotive business.

events.title: U.S. ports face record import container volume
events.description: Retailers are frontloading imports to avoid anticipated August tariff increases, pushing major U.S. container ports toward a new all-time volume record this month.

events.title: Maersk resumes Suez Canal route via MECL service
events.description: Maersk is expanding its return to the Suez Canal, with its MECL service now transiting the canal again, linking India, the Middle East, and the U.S. East Coast. This marks a significant shift in routing amid ongoing Red Sea disruptions.

events.title: U.S. escalates strikes on Iran after attacks on commercial ships
events.description: The U.S. has escalated military strikes against Iran, targeting coastal defenses, missile sites, and naval infrastructure. Iran has warned that shipping will only resume under 'Iranian arrangements,' raising concerns about potential disruptions to maritime freight flows in the region.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian tankers, worsening fuel shortages
events.description: Ukraine claimed to have struck 12 Russian tankers in southern Russia, escalating attacks that are deepening a nationwide gasoline shortage and disrupting fuel supply chains.

events.title: Hormuz Strait tanker traffic near standstill due to attacks
events.description: Oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has come to a near standstill as shipping risks escalate following U.S. airstrikes on Iran and subsequent retaliation by Tehran in the Gulf.

events.title: ONE launches India North America Express service
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) is replacing its WIN service with the new India North America Express (INE) from August 2026 to improve network stability and sailing frequency between West India, West Asia, and the US East Coast.

events.title: Maersk returns MECL service to Suez route
events.description: Maersk has announced the return of its MECL service to the trans-Suez route via the Red Sea, following successful transit and prior adjustments to its AE15 service. This marks a continued shift back to traditional Suez Canal shipping routes.

events.title: Monsoon storms disrupt India's west coast ports
events.description: Monsoon storms have caused significant disruptions at Nhava Sheva Port and surrounding container terminals, including suspension of operations and vessel backlogs. PSA’s Bharat Mumbai Container Terminals (BMCT) was among the worst affected.

events.title: US CPSC mandates eFiling for import compliance
events.description: The US Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) launched a mandatory eFiling program requiring importers to submit compliance certificates electronically through ACE at the moment of entry, adding a new layer of pre-arrival documentation and increasing data requirements at the border.

events.title: Air freight capacity shifts globally as airlines reposition fleets
events.description: Freighter operators are repositioning aircraft in response to geopolitical tensions, regulatory changes, and capacity shortages. Recent data shows significant shifts in cargo capacity, including a 12% increase between Europe and South America.

events.title: Southern Africa supply chain disruption impacts trade
events.description: Southern Africa's trade growth is being hindered by constrained port, vessel, and inland logistics networks, exacerbated by conflict in the Persian Gulf. South Africa's citrus exports have dropped by 24% year-to-date due to these disruptions.

events.title: Gulftainer expands Khor Fakkan logistics capacity
events.description: Gulftainer is expanding the Khor Fakkan port to transform it into a one-stop logistics hub, creating new business groups to enhance its logistics offerings.

events.title: Concerns over potential tolls at Strait of Malacca
events.description: Energy market participants are concerned that Iran's influence and potential toll demands could extend to the Strait of Malacca, a critical global trade and energy chokepoint. This follows Iran's reported actions near the Strait of Hormuz and raises fears of disruptions to shipping flows.

events.title: Russia bans diesel exports after Ukrainian drone attacks
events.description: Russia has reportedly banned diesel exports to prevent domestic shortages following multiple Ukrainian drone attacks on its oil refineries. The new policy expands previous restrictions and aims to secure domestic fuel supplies.

events.title: Russian refinery attacks disrupt product exports, boost crude shipments
events.description: Attacks on Russian refining infrastructure in June reduced refined product exports and caused domestic shortages, leading to a 9% monthly increase in seaborne crude exports to 4.414 million barrels/day.

events.title: Hormuz crisis boosts oil tanker trade
events.description: A South Korean shipping tycoon capitalized on the Hormuz crisis by acquiring oil tankers, profiting from increased demand due to disruptions in global oil markets caused by the Iran conflict.

events.title: Tankers attacked in Hormuz NCAGS corridor
events.description: The IRGC attacked two tankers on the NCAGS-approved route in the Strait of Hormuz on July 7, prompting rerouting of six vessels. The attack involved a Qatari LNG carrier and a Saudi-owned ULCC.

events.title: U.S.-Iran clashes near Hormuz Strait
events.description: The U.S. and Iran continued exchanging strikes overnight, escalating tensions in the Hormuz Strait, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Poland-Ukraine supply chain integration challenges
events.description: Gdańsk handles nearly a third of Ukraine's container trade, but integration is hindered by rail gauge differences and customs inefficiencies rather than port capacity limitations.

events.title: U.S. court upholds FMC detention fee ruling
events.description: A U.S. appeals court upheld the Federal Maritime Commission's (FMC) decision that detention fees charged during a three-day port closure were unreasonable, impacting shipping and logistics operations.

events.title: Korea probes container sales cartel
events.description: The Korea Fair Trade Commission is investigating four major container manufacturers for alleged collusion to limit output and raise prices between 2019 and 2024, following similar allegations by the US Department of Justice.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian tankers and refinery
events.location: Kerch Strait and Sea of Azov
events.description: Ukraine has intensified attacks on Russian tankers in the Kerch Strait and Sea of Azov, while also striking a major refinery, worsening Russia's fuel crisis and disrupting oil infrastructure.

events.title: Hormuz Strait traffic near standstill due to US-Iran conflict
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill following US strikes on Iran and retaliatory missile and drone attacks by Iran on Kuwait and Bahrain. The situation escalated as President Trump declared the ceasefire between the US and Iran 'over.'

events.title: New Zealand loses last flagged container ship
events.description: Swire Shipping is consulting on plans to end Pacifica Shipping's coastal container service, which includes the New Zealand-flagged vessel Moana Chief. This would leave New Zealand without any domestic-flagged container ships.

events.title: U.S. airstrikes hit Iranian ports and military bases
events.description: The U.S. launched airstrikes against Iranian ports and military bases, which could disrupt regional shipping and supply chain operations. The strikes may affect freight flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Hutchison Ports deploys automated cranes at Lázaro Cárdenas
events.description: Hutchison Ports has integrated 12 new Automated Rubber Tired Gantry cranes at the Specialized Container Terminal I in Lázaro Cárdenas, enhancing operational efficiency and capacity.

events.title: Texas Port Expands Automotive Imports with Infrastructure Upgrades
events.description: Port Freeport is expanding its automotive import capabilities through a new deal and infrastructure upgrades, including a $11 million grant. The port also celebrated milestones with Volkswagen and Höegh.

events.title: FMC authority to challenge detention fees upheld
events.description: A federal appeals court upheld the Federal Maritime Commission's authority to strike down unreasonable container detention fees imposed by ocean carriers, reinforcing regulatory oversight in the shipping industry.

events.title: Attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The IMO Secretary-General condemned recent attacks on commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting the risks to seafarers and global shipping flows due to ongoing geopolitical tensions.

events.title: US and Iran exchange airstrikes, sanctions reimposed
events.description: The US and Iran have exchanged airstrikes, escalating tensions. Additionally, Washington has reimposed crude oil sales sanctions on Iran, potentially disrupting regional energy trade and shipping routes.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Transits Partially Resume
events.description: The Hormuz Strait transits are gradually resuming following the Islamabad MOU signed on 17 June, which marks a tentative de-escalation of the Iran conflict. However, full normalisation is not expected before year-end, and the prolonged closure has already reshaped global trade patterns.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Maritime Risk Reminder
events.description: Recent disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz highlight ongoing maritime risks beyond immediate incidents, affecting global freight flows and underlining the region's strategic importance.

events.title: Ukraine strikes 21 vessels in Sea of Azov
events.description: Ukrainian drone forces have reportedly struck 21 vessels, including nine tankers, in the Sea of Azov over 72 hours, intensifying attacks near Crimea and disrupting regional maritime activity.

events.title: Antwerp Left Bank Container Cluster Approved
events.description: The Flemish Government has approved the draft project decision for the Left Bank Container Cluster, part of a plan to expand the Port of Antwerp-Bruges' container handling capacity by 7 million TEUs to accommodate growing traffic.

events.title: Maersk adds Nordic inland fuel surcharge due to Middle East situation
events.description: Maersk is introducing a temporary Emergency Inland Fuel/Energy Surcharge for inland shipments across the Nordic region, effective 8 July 2026, due to increased fuel costs linked to the evolving situation in the Middle East.

events.title: Russia bans diesel exports due to Ukrainian strikes
events.description: Russia imposed a diesel export ban to stabilize domestic fuel supplies after Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries caused shortages and price spikes.

events.title: Bosphorus Closure for Yacht Race
events.description: The Bosphorus will be closed to international vessel traffic on July 11, 2026, for the TAYK–AKPA Turkish Naval Forces Cup International Yacht Race, disrupting freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Port Hedland iron ore export strike threatens disruptions
events.description: Unions have announced an eight-hour work stoppage at BHP's Port Hedland operations, one of Australia's largest iron ore export hubs, potentially disrupting freight flows.

events.title: VLCC WEDYAN Incident Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Bahri confirms a Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC) WEDYAN was involved in an incident near the Strait of Hormuz on 7 July 2026. All crew members are safe, and the cargo remains secure.

events.title: Dar es Salaam Port Discharge Times Reduced by 90%
events.description: DP World Dar es Salaam has significantly improved port efficiency, reducing cargo discharge times by over 90% since April 2024, from over 300 hours to under 28 hours.

events.title: Mexico truck driver shortage worsens
events.description: Mexico is experiencing a severe shortage of truck drivers, with one of the highest vacancy rates globally, according to the International Road Transport Union, threatening freight movement and supply chain efficiency.

events.title: High container rates due to Iran conflict
events.description: Container rates on the trans-Pacific route have reached nearly $9,000 amid rising tensions in the Iran conflict. The situation is contributing to ongoing supply chain pressures despite lower fuel costs.

events.title: UP-Norfolk Southern Merger Regulatory Deadline Approaches
events.description: The proposed $85 billion merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern faces a key regulatory decision by the Surface Transportation Board on July 27, with significant implications for rail freight competition and supply chain operations.

events.title: EU removes de minimis exemption for low-value imports
events.description: The EU has ended the de minimis exemption for low-value imports, requiring parcels under €150 to pay a €3 per category duty. This policy shift could impact freight flows and boost logistics hubs like Glasgow Prestwick Airport.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz near-closure and Red Sea crisis impact
events.description: CMA CGM's CEO Rodolphe Saadé highlights the ongoing disruption caused by the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea crisis, emphasizing the need for logistics resilience in a fragmented trading environment.

events.title: Rising intermodal congestion and drayage costs in US imports
events.description: ITS Logistics warns that increasing import volumes and over-the-road rates are causing intermodal congestion, slower train speeds, and rising drayage and transport costs for cargo moving inland from US ocean gateways.

events.title: U.S. strikes Iran, IMO advises ships to avoid Hormuz
events.description: The U.S. launched strikes against Iranian military targets following attacks on vessels, prompting the IMO to urge ships to avoid the Strait of Hormuz due to heightened security risks.

events.title: HMM launches hub-and-spoke service in Africa
events.description: HMM has launched its first hub-and-spoke network service, MA2, connecting Spain and West Africa via Algeciras. The service uses feeder ships to link major ports, enhancing regional freight connectivity.

events.title: Box ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Malta-flagged container ship San Antonio, carrying 2,824 TEU, was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz on 5 May, causing serious damage, crew injuries, and raising fears of total cargo loss for Indian shippers.

events.title: India-North Europe trade faces capacity constraints
events.description: Booming westbound demand on the India/Middle East-Europe trade route is meeting tight capacity, causing severe delays and requiring bookings four-to-six weeks in advance.

events.title: Damaged Qatari LNG Tanker Near Hormuz
events.description: The Qatari LNG tanker Al Rekayyat is stationary near the Strait of Hormuz and awaiting salvage after being struck by a projectile and experiencing a fire on board.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transit levels still below normal
events.description: Transit through the Strait of Hormuz has increased but remains significantly below pre-crisis levels, with vessel traffic still prone to sudden disruptions, according to recent Reuters data.

events.title: Iranian attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iranian attacks on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, including an LNG carrier and an oil tanker, caused oil prices to spike. The incidents have raised concerns over regional stability and global energy supply chains.

events.title: BHP Port Hedland Iron Ore Terminal Strike
events.description: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland iron ore terminal in Western Australia are planning an eight-hour strike on July 16, raising concerns over potential supply disruptions. The industrial action involves operators and maintenance workers.

events.title: Japan bans jet fuel exports to Russia, including via third countries
events.description: Japan clarified that its ban on jet fuel exports to Russia includes shipments through third countries and transfers at sea. The move follows reports that Russia was set to receive a jet fuel cargo from Japan via traders.

events.title: Mitsubishi expands vehicle distribution to Port Freeport
events.description: Mitsubishi Motors North America has launched operations at Port Freeport in partnership with AMPORTS, expanding its U.S. vehicle distribution network to include the Gulf Coast and Midwest.

events.title: CMA CGM increases PSS on Indian Subcontinent shipments
events.description: CMA CGM has announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) of $500 per container on dry cargo shipments from the Indian Subcontinent to Europe, the Mediterranean, and Latin America, effective later this month.

events.title: China detains Panama-flagged ships, targets Chinese carriers in US
events.description: The US Federal Maritime Commission has raised concerns over China's continued detention of Panama-flagged ships, suggesting the actions are retaliatory and could lead to consequences for Chinese-controlled carriers operating in US trade.

events.title: Tanker attacks escalate crisis in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Three tanker attacks in 24 hours, including a Qatari LNG carrier and a Saudi-linked VLCC, triggered US strikes on Iran and a sharp escalation in the Gulf, pushing commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz back into crisis mode.

events.title: Freighter collides with fishing boat off Vietnam
events.description: A freighter collided with a Vietnamese fishing boat, resulting in one death and 16 rescues. The incident occurred off the southern coast of Vietnam.

events.title: EU imposes new customs charges on low-value UK exports
events.description: New EU customs charges of €3 per parcel now apply to low-value shipments (€150 or less) from the UK to the EU, effective 1 July 2026. The Rhenus Group has warned UK exporters to adjust their operations accordingly.

events.title: U.S. Revokes Iran Oil Waiver, Launches Strikes After Hormuz Attacks
events.description: The Trump administration revoked Iran's oil sanctions waiver and launched military strikes following renewed attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint.

events.title: Iran Mines Strait of Hormuz to Control Shipping
events.description: Iran is mining the Strait of Hormuz to funnel commercial shipping into sea lanes near its shores, aiming to control traffic and collect tolls, according to the U.S. Navy's top military official.

events.title: Oil tanker hit by projectile in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An oil tanker was struck by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The British military confirmed the incident, while Iranian state media suggested the attack targeted a Qatari-linked LNG tanker.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces Neopanamax draft limits
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority will lower the maximum draft for Neopanamax vessels to 14.94 meters effective 24 July due to El Nino conditions, impacting vessel capacity and transit efficiency.

events.title: Super Typhoon Bavi threatens China ports and Pacific vessel supply
events.description: Super Typhoon Bavi is moving toward the Taiwan–China shipping corridor, risking short-term disruption to vessel routing, port operations, and commodity flows. The typhoon's intensity and trajectory could impact China port operations and Pacific vessel supply.

events.title: IRGC attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacked multiple ships in the Strait of Hormuz on July 6-7, signaling a hardline stance and raising concerns over regional security and maritime trade disruptions.

events.title: US revokes Iran's oil sales license
events.description: The U.S. Treasury revoked Iran's general license to sell oil, citing retaliation for recent tanker attacks. This move could disrupt Iranian oil exports and affect global oil supply chains.

events.title: Mountain Valley Express ceases operations
events.description: Less-than-truckload carrier Mountain Valley Express has confirmed it has ceased operations, potentially impacting regional freight logistics and customer shipping networks.

events.title: Hormuz Strikes Hinder De-mining Efforts
events.description: Fresh attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted the UK and France's de-mining mission, threatening progress in clearing mines from this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery
events.description: Ukrainian forces conducted a drone strike on an oil refinery in Nizhnekamsk, Russia, over 2,500 km behind the front lines, aiming to disrupt Russian military logistics and energy infrastructure.

events.title: Qatari tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A Qatari tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. Qatar condemned the incident, calling it a threat to international navigation and energy supplies.

events.title: Tankers Struck Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Two tankers were struck from the air near the Omani coast as they exited the Strait of Hormuz. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed control over the strait, raising concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to global oil shipments.

events.title: Ukraine attacks fuel tankers in Crimea
events.description: Ukraine reports hitting eight fuel tankers in Crimea, targeting industrial infrastructure and fuel supplies in the occupied region.

events.title: Malacca Strait security reassurance amid geopolitical concerns
events.description: The Malacca Strait, a critical global shipping route, received public reassurance of its security amid comparisons to the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting concerns over potential disruptions.

events.title: LNG Tanker at Risk of Explosion Near Hormuz
events.description: A Qatari LNG tanker, 'Al Rekayyat,' is at risk of exploding after an attack near the Strait of Hormuz. A Saudi-flagged crude oil tanker was also damaged in the same incident, reportedly after Iran fired missiles at ships in the area.

events.title: Tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A third commercial tanker has been attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, with UK Maritime Trade Operations reporting the vessel was struck by an uncrewed aerial device. The Joint Maritime Information Centre (JMIC) has warned of a 'severe' threat in the region.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian 'shadow fleet' tankers
events.description: Ukrainian drones have attacked a dozen Russian 'shadow fleet' tankers delivering fuel to Crimea, intensifying efforts to disrupt supply lines to the Russian-occupied peninsula.

events.title: Cargo ship loses steering in Bass Strait due to crew training gaps
events.description: A cargo ship in Bass Strait lost steering for over eight hours due to a mechanical fault and crew's inability to use the backup system, highlighting training deficiencies.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd increases freight rates for India-Bangladesh to Europe
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has raised base freight rates by $2,000 per container for shipments from India and Bangladesh to Northern Europe, the Mediterranean, and the Black Sea, effective from 1 August 2026.

events.title: Gemini returns to Suez Canal route
events.description: The Gemini vessel is returning to the Suez Canal route as part of Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd's AE15 service restoration, shifting from the Cape of Good Hope route following a security assessment.

events.title: Global container shipping reliability drops due to port congestion
events.description: Global container shipping schedule reliability declined in May as port congestion continued to impact on-time performance, with carriers achieving an average reliability of 39%. Average delays increased to 3.4 days, signaling ongoing challenges in the supply chain.

events.title: BAL deploys transpacific vessel on China-US route
events.description: Chinese shipowner BAL Container Line is re-entering liner trades with the deployment of its 14,400 teu vessel BAL Athena on an ad hoc China-US West Coast service starting 24 July.

events.title: Dispute over bidding rules for Santos Tecon10 terminal
events.description: Brazil’s Antaq is pushing for a two-stage bidding process for the Tecon10 container terminal in Santos, conflicting with government efforts to remove restrictions that block existing operators from the first round of bids.

events.title: Attacks Near Strait of Hormuz Raise Supply Fears
events.description: Oil prices increased after attacks on vessels near the Strait of Hormuz raised concerns over potential disruptions to this vital energy shipping route.

events.title: Security concerns rise in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Renewed security concerns in the Strait of Hormuz are causing oil prices to rise, highlighting ongoing risks to global energy supply chains.

events.title: Japanese ships depart Hormuz near Iran
events.description: Eight vessels operated by Mitsui OSK Lines, including five oil supertankers, are exiting the Strait of Hormuz via a passage near Iran. These ships are among the last trapped tankers to clear the strategic waterway.

events.title: Russian tankers increase crude shipments amid flag registry clampdowns
events.description: Russian tanker operators have increased crude oil shipments from Russian ports as non-G7 fleet operators retreat due to flag registry clampdowns. This shift reflects a policy-driven disruption in global tanker operations and trade flows.

events.title: Tanker struck by projectile in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was set ablaze after being struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil and natural gas shipments. The incident adds to ongoing tensions in the region and risks further disrupting vital maritime trade flows.

events.title: Super Typhoon Bavi threatens Pacific shipping lanes
events.description: Super Typhoon Bavi, currently a Category 5 storm, is expected to impact the Taiwan Strait on July 10th, potentially disrupting vessel traffic and commodity supply chains in one of the world's busiest shipping lanes.

events.title: Iran attacks LNG carrier in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran's military forces attacked an LNG carrier in the Strait of Hormuz, marking a resumption of attacks on merchant shipping in the strategic waterway.

events.title: Maersk revises peak season surcharge for Latin America
events.description: Maersk is adjusting its Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) for shipments from Far East Asia to Latin America, including Mexico, the West Coast of South America, Central America, and the Caribbean. The new surcharge will take effect on 10 July 2026 for cargo from Far East Asia, excluding South Korea.

events.title: Qatari LNG Ship Struck in Strait of Hormuz
events.location: Strait of Hormuz, near Omani coast
events.description: A Qatari LNG ship was struck by a projectile near the Omani coast as it exited the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about regional security and testing a US-Iran agreement meant to prevent such attacks.

events.title: Japanese tankers resume Mideast crude transport via Hormuz
events.description: Two Japanese-owned supertankers carrying Saudi oil are exiting the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a recovery in Japan's Middle East crude supply after earlier disruptions.

events.title: Projectile strike near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was struck by an unknown projectile late Monday while transiting just east of the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about the security and stability of this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Israeli PM and Defense Minister Oppose ZIM Sale
events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz have urged the cancellation of the proposed sale of ZIM Integrated Shipping Services to Hapag-Lloyd and FIMI, citing national security concerns.

events.title: Sulfur-laden bulkers face corrosion risks near Hormuz
events.description: Bulk carriers transporting sulfur through the Strait of Hormuz are experiencing delays, increasing the risk of cargo corrosion. The issue highlights vulnerabilities in freight flows through this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Tanker struck by projectile near Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was struck by an unknown projectile late Monday while transiting just east of the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a fire. The incident is the latest security threat in a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Stranded sulphur cargoes risk corrosion in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Bulk carriers carrying elemental sulphur in the Strait of Hormuz are at risk of structural corrosion due to prolonged delays at anchorage, exceeding standard cargo protection periods. Brookes Bell warns of significant safety and operational concerns for shipowners.

events.title: 44 Seafarers Held by Pirates in Somali Waters
events.location: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden
events.description: IMO Secretary-General has called for the urgent release of 44 seafarers held by pirates in Somali waters. The captives are being held after a surge in attacks on ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.

events.title: Japan-linked vessels exit Strait of Hormuz after months stranded
events.description: Ten Japan-linked vessels, including six crude carriers and two chemical tankers, are exiting the Strait of Hormuz after being stranded for months in the Gulf, according to shipping data and media reports.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian tankers, ports, and refineries
events.description: Ukraine conducted a large-scale coordinated attack on Russian tankers, ports, and refineries, disrupting energy infrastructure and potentially affecting supply chain operations.

events.title: Somali Pirates Hijack Three Ships
events.description: The International Maritime Organization is calling for the release of three ships hijacked by Somali pirates, with dozens of seafarers being held for ransom.

events.title: Iran-Linked Tankers Detained in India
events.description: Three Iran-linked tankers detained by Indian courts were driven toward shore during a monsoon, prompting a Coast Guard rescue mission on July 5.

events.title: Vessel movements suspended at Paradip Port due to rough seas
events.description: Inward and outward vessel movements at Paradip Port have been suspended due to heavy swell and rough sea conditions. Cargo operations for vessels already alongside berths continue unaffected.

events.title: Cargo ship attacked in Red Sea off Yemen
events.location: Red Sea southwest of Hodeida, Yemen
events.description: A cargo ship was attacked 30 nautical miles southwest of Hodeida, Yemen, an area controlled by Houthi rebels. The Iran-backed group has vowed to resume attacks in the Red Sea, raising concerns over regional shipping safety.

events.title: IMO Enhances Red Sea Navigation Safety
events.location: Red Sea and Horn of Africa
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) is advancing its Red Sea Project to improve maritime security and situational awareness in the Red Sea and Horn of Africa, addressing ongoing risks to shipping in the region.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces Neopanamax draft limits
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority announced two more decreases in Neopanamax draft levels, following a prior reduction in June. The changes aim to manage water levels and could impact vessel capacity and transit efficiency.

events.title: Maersk and Hapag Shift Route to Suez-Red Sea Transit
events.description: Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd are shifting one shipping route back to the Suez Canal and Red Sea transit, indicating a strategic adjustment in response to ongoing disruptions in other shipping corridors.

events.title: AE15 service rerouted through Suez Canal
events.description: Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd have returned their AE15 Gemini Cooperation service to the Suez Canal route, following a security assessment. The reroute takes effect with the sailing of the Majestic Maersk.

events.title: Mexico enforces new customs value declarations
events.description: Mexico will begin enforcing electronic customs value declarations on August 1, prompting warnings from CrimsonLogic about potential exposure of poor supply chain data.

events.title: Maritime Security Concerns in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The IMO Council highlighted ongoing maritime security issues in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, with continued instability and risks of disruption to freight flows.

events.title: Panama Canal Tightens Draft Restrictions
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority will reduce the maximum draft for Neopanamax locks later this summer due to a strengthening El Niño, impacting vessel transits and cargo capacity.

events.title: UAE resumes oil flows via Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United Arab Emirates has resumed nearly pre-war levels of oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz following a covert initiative, as the US and Iran reached an interim peace deal.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz geopolitical uncertainty impacts oil flows
events.description: Lingering geopolitical uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz continues to affect oil markets, despite OPEC+ plans to increase output. The situation remains a key risk to global energy supply chains.

events.title: Saudi Arabia increases oil shipments via Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Saudi Arabia has increased oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz following the U.S.-Iran agreement to reopen the sea lane. According to Kpler, about 34 million barrels have been shipped through the strait since June 17.

events.title: Tianjin Port sets new draft record with ultra-large bulk carrier
events.description: Tianjin Port in China accommodated the ultra-large bulk carrier Zhonghai Ronghua with a draft of 22.18 meters, setting a new record for the deepest-draft vessel to dock at the port.

events.title: Middle East tensions impact Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Despite a recent 60-day peace agreement, the situation in the Middle East remains fragile, with US and Iran exchanging strikes. Freight flow through the Strait of Hormuz is increasing post-attacks but remains disrupted.

events.title: EU RED III policy concerns in West Med bunker markets
events.description: Gibraltar Port Authority is concerned about the impact of the EU's Renewable Energy Directive III on West Mediterranean bunker markets, potentially shifting refueling to non-European ports with limited decarbonization benefits.

events.title: Israeli Defense Ministry opposes Zim sale to Hapag-Lloyd
events.description: Israel's Defense Ministry has formally opposed the proposed $4.2 billion sale of Zim to Hapag-Lloyd, citing concerns over maritime security and supply chain impacts. The move adds to government scrutiny of the transaction.

events.title: ONE launches Mediterranean Africa Express service
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) has launched the Mediterranean Africa Express (MAX) service, a weekly route connecting West Africa to global trade routes, starting July 7, 2026. The service aims to enhance direct freight links between West Africa and major global markets.

events.title: Maersk resumes Melbourne Star seasonal export service
events.description: Maersk is resuming its seasonal Melbourne Star export service to support Australia's peak export season, with weekly calls at DP World Terminal Melbourne and connections to Southeast Asian transshipment hubs.

events.title: Dedicated cargo flights to Chicago launched
events.description: Ceva, DSV, and Kuehne+Nagel have launched dedicated international cargo flights to the Chicago area through agreements with major cargo airlines to enhance freight services.

events.title: Potential Suez Canal shipping return due to Persian Gulf peace deal
events.description: A major carrier partnership may shift global shipping back to the Suez Canal following a peace deal in the Persian Gulf, signaling a potential easing of route disruptions.

events.title: Asia-Europe ocean freight rate split nears record
events.description: Disruption at the Strait of Hormuz has caused a near-record pricing gap between Asia-North Europe and Asia-Mediterranean container services, distorting global freight markets.

events.title: Fatal accident halts JNPA Nhava Sheva terminal operations
events.description: Operations at Nhava Sheva Freeport Terminal in India’s JNPA port were halted following a fatal accident involving container collapse during sludge removal. The terminal is expected to remain closed until tomorrow.

events.title: Jeddah port congestion and Suez Canal service update
events.description: Jeddah port continues to experience 4-6 hour delays, impacting shipments. Meanwhile, the Gemini carriers, including Maersk, have resumed the AE15 service through the Suez Canal, signaling potential easing of regional freight bottlenecks.

events.title: Israeli officials block ZIM sale to Hapag-Lloyd
events.description: Israeli government officials, including Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, have opposed ZIM Integrated Shipping Services' proposed sale to Hapag-Lloyd, citing strategic concerns over Qatari and Saudi ownership stakes. The decision has caused ZIM's stock to drop significantly.

events.title: Maersk resumes Suez route for AE15 service
events.description: Maersk, in collaboration with Hapag-Lloyd, announced a return to the trans-Suez route for the AE15 service, following security assessments in the Red Sea. This marks a gradual shift back from the Cape of Good Hope route.

events.title: Indonesia and Singapore reaffirm Strait of Malacca free passage
events.description: Indonesia and Singapore have reaffirmed their commitment to maintaining free passage through the Strait of Malacca amid renewed concerns over global navigation security due to Middle East tensions.

events.title: AE15 service resumes Suez Canal route
events.description: Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd are resuming container shipping through the Suez Canal with their AE15 service, marking a cautious return after previously using the Cape of Good Hope route.

events.title: 44 Seafarers Held by Somali Pirates
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has called for urgent action to secure the release of 44 seafarers held captive by pirates in Somali waters, highlighting a critical security threat to maritime operations.

events.title: Tank Barge Explosion on Paraguay River
events.description: A tank barge exploded on the Paraguay River, killing five workers. The incident occurred on Saturday morning and could impact river freight operations.

events.title: CMA CGM adds peak season surcharges on key trade lanes
events.description: CMA CGM announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) of $500 per container on trade lanes from the Indian Subcontinent and China, effective July 2026, impacting routes to Latin America and other destinations.

events.title: Shipping vulnerability in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Industry sentiment highlights ongoing vulnerability of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, despite recent diplomatic efforts. The latest incident reinforces concerns about potential disruptions to global freight flows in the region.

events.title: CNC launches direct Shantou–Thailand/Cambodia route
events.description: CNC has revamped its JTX service to include the first direct connection from Shantou to Thailand and Cambodia, enhancing Intra-Asia freight connectivity and offering a new route option between China and Southeast Asia.

events.title: Tehran-linked ship grounded in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A 2006-built 1,574 TEU container ship, the Arista, which is linked to Iran and sanctioned by the US, ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz on 1 July 2026. The incident could disrupt critical shipping flows through this key global chokepoint.

events.title: Cargo vessel attacked off Yemen in Red Sea
events.description: A cargo vessel was attacked by unknown armed assailants approximately 30 nautical miles off Yemen in the Red Sea, raising concerns about ongoing maritime security risks in the region.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces neopanamax draught limits
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority is reducing the maximum authorised draught for neopanamax vessels to 14.94 meters, signaling renewed water scarcity concerns at this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: US courts redefine 'package' under COGSA
events.description: Recent US court decisions, particularly in the Second Circuit, are shifting the interpretation of 'package' under COGSA, prioritizing contractual definitions in bills of lading. This could significantly impact liability limits for carriers and affect claims handling in the shipping industry.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz vessel transits drop to four-day low
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz fell to a four-day low on July 2 due to ongoing regional tensions. Iran-linked vessels accounted for nearly 60% of crossings, according to S&P Global Energy vessel tracking data.

events.title: Russia's crude export flow shift impacts tanker market
events.description: Russia’s reduced crude processing capacity is increasing seaborne exports, altering cargo flows and impacting the tanker market as the Ukraine war enters its fifth year.

events.title: VLCC freight market shifts due to geopolitical risks
events.description: The VLCC freight market is transitioning from pricing based on risk to pricing based on vessel utilisation, as geopolitical tensions persist in the Strait of Hormuz. This shift reflects ongoing uncertainty and evolving dynamics in global oil shipping routes.

events.title: Cargo ship attacked near Hodeidah, Yemen
events.description: A cargo ship reported an attack off the coast of Yemen near Hodeidah, where hostilities between Houthi rebels and international forces continue.

events.title: Port of Galveston opens new cargo berth
events.description: The Port of Galveston opened a new cargo berth with the arrival of its first Wallenius Wilhelmsen cargo vessel on July 2, 2026, expanding its capacity for freight operations.

events.title: Sailors Stranded Behind Hormuz Due To Conflict
events.description: Over 8,000 sailors remain stranded behind the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing disruptions, likely due to geopolitical tensions. The situation highlights a critical chokepoint disruption affecting global shipping and crew movements.

events.title: Cargo Ship Attacked Off Yemen
events.description: A cargo vessel in the Red Sea reported an attack by armed assailants, highlighting ongoing security risks in the region and adding to supply chain disruptions.

events.title: European Hormuz Naval Coalition Disbands
events.description: The European-led naval coalition, established to protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, has disbanded without formal notice, raising concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: Hannibal launches Melzo–Rotterdam intermodal rail service
events.description: Hannibal, a Contship Group operator, has launched a new intermodal rail service between Melzo (Rail Hub Milano) and Rotterdam Europoort, enhancing freight connectivity and access to one of Europe's largest logistics hubs.

events.title: Red Sea risk persists despite freight recovery
events.description: Freight flows in the Hormuz corridor are recovering, but risks in the Red Sea remain, highlighting ongoing safety concerns despite improved traffic conditions.

events.title: CMA CGM increases Asia-Europe-North Africa FAK rates
events.location: Asia to Europe and North Africa
events.description: CMA CGM has announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for container shipments from Asia to North Europe, the Mediterranean, and North Africa, effective from 15 July to 31 July 2026. The rate changes include $4,100 for 20-foot containers to North Europe.

events.title: Iran and Qatar resume maritime trade
events.description: Maritime trade between Iran and Qatar has resumed after a five-month suspension, following an interim deal between the two countries. The resumption could impact regional freight flows and trade dynamics in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: Rising Violence Against Truckers in Mexico Borderlands
events.description: While cargo theft in the Mexico Borderlands has decreased, attacks on truckers have become deadlier, raising concerns about safety and security for freight operations in the region.

events.title: CMA CGM launches Iberimar shipping service
events.description: CMA CGM has launched the Iberimar service, a weekly shipping route connecting the Iberian Peninsula's Atlantic coast with Morocco and the Strait of Gibraltar. The service includes calls at Tangier, Algeciras, Vigo, Leixões, Lisbon, Casablanca, and Agadir.

events.title: KMTC merges JKW3 and JKW4 services
events.description: KMTC is consolidating its fortnightly JKW3 and JKW4 Busan-Japan services into a single weekly JKW3 loop. The change removes Nagasaki from the rotation and adjusts port frequencies.

events.title: Diamond Line launches Spain-Morocco feeder service
events.description: Diamond Line has launched a new Spain-Morocco shuttle service to enhance cargo links between Valencia and Casablanca, operating on a fixed seven-day schedule.

events.title: Akkon Lines expands EAS service to Mogadishu
events.description: Akkon Lines has added Mogadishu, Somalia, to its East Africa–Indian Subcontinent (EAS) service, operating fortnightly with 1,700 TEU vessels. The route now includes Nhava Sheva, Mundra, Karachi, Dar es Salaam, Mombasa, and Mogadishu.

events.title: CMA CGM Ship Damaged by Missile in Hormuz Strait
events.description: A CMA CGM container ship was struck by a missile in the Strait of Hormuz in early May, sustaining severe damage that may lead to its scrapping.

events.title: Ukrainian drone attack hits St Petersburg oil terminal
events.description: A major Ukrainian drone attack hit a port and oil infrastructure in St Petersburg, Russia, causing supply chain disruptions to oil logistics.

events.title: Maersk raises ECS for Indian Subcontinent to West Coast Latin America
events.location: Indian Subcontinent to West Coast Latin America
events.description: Maersk has increased Emergency Contingency Surcharges (ECS) for shipments from the Indian Subcontinent, including North West India, Pakistan, South and East India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and the Maldives, to the West Coast of Latin America, the Caribbean, and Central America.

events.title: Ruscon uses Northern Sea Route for China to St. Petersburg
events.description: Ruscon, part of the Delo Group, is utilizing the Northern Sea Route for westbound container shipments from China to St. Petersburg, reducing transit times to around 30 days compared to the Suez Canal route.

events.title: Gulftainer launches Umm Qasr feeder service
events.description: Gulftainer has launched a dedicated feeder service connecting the UAE and Umm Qasr, Iraq, through its shipping arm GT Lines. This marks an expansion of Gulftainer's integrated logistics operations in the region.

events.title: Middle East tanker flows recover post-ceasefire
events.description: In June, the Middle East tanker market showed cautious recovery as a temporary US-Iran ceasefire boosted regional transit activity, according to Gibson Shipbrokers Ltd.

events.title: Iran warns oil tankers on Hormuz routes
events.description: Iran’s joint military command warned oil tankers to use approved routes in the Strait of Hormuz or face a 'forceful response,' increasing tensions in a key global energy supply route.

events.title: Gulf of Hormuz Fleet Movement and Mine Constraints
events.description: Over 550 merchant vessels are preparing to exit the Gulf of Hormuz, with crude flows exceeding 10 million barrels per day. The central shipping channel remains constrained by an estimated 80 mines.

events.title: Intra-Asia container freight rates decline
events.description: Drewry's Intra-Asia Container Index fell 4% last week, with notable declines on routes like Shanghai–Busan and Ho Chi Minh City. This follows a second consecutive week of rate reductions, indicating easing demand during the early peak season.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Impacts Shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, placing shipping at the 'frontline' of geo-political tensions and disrupting global freight flows.

events.title: Asia-US container rates surge due to impending tariffs
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US West Coast have surged by 8% as importers rush to move cargo ahead of potential tariff increases. This rate increase reflects trade policy uncertainty and its impact on freight flows.

events.title: Port of Galveston opens new cargo berth
events.description: The Port of Galveston officially opened a new cargo berth on July 2, with the first call by a Wallenius Wilhelmsen cargo carrier. The development supports increased roll-on/roll-off cargo operations at the port.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd increases freight rates for India/Pakistan to Europe
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has raised ocean freight rates for shipments from India and Pakistan to Northern Europe and the Mediterranean, effective from 1 August 2026. The base rate for 20-foot dry containers from Nhava Sheva and Mundra will increase by US$2,000 per container.

events.title: Emirates deploys first Boeing 777-300ERSF freighter
events.description: Emirates has deployed its first converted Boeing 777-300ERSF freighter, registered A6-EBK, into commercial service. The aircraft carried over 100 tonnes of cargo on its inaugural flight from Hong Kong to Dubai, expanding the airline's cargo capabilities.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Security Concerns
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but uncertainty remains over the security of safe passage amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and US-Iran peace talks.

events.title: Container rates rise sharply on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes
events.description: The Drewry World Container Index increased by 9% to $4,530 per 40ft container, driven by rising spot rates on transpacific and Asia-Europe trades. Rates from Shanghai to New York climbed 11% to $7,902, indicating significant industry stress.

events.title: GENMA supplies RTGs for Fujairah Port expansion
events.description: GENMA has secured an order to supply eight rubber-tyred gantry (RTG) cranes for the Port of Fujairah in the UAE, supporting AD Ports Group's terminal expansion. The cranes will be customized for the region's harsh environmental conditions.

events.title: Mundra Port Requires Prior Acceptance for Vessels
events.description: Prior acceptance is required for vessels calling at Mundra Port, India, effective 03 Jul 2026. Without approval, vessel call notifications (VCNs) will not be processed, except for container vessels under certain conditions.

events.title: Iran rejects Hormuz Strait demining plan
events.description: Iran has rejected a demining proposal from France and Oman for the Strait of Hormuz, which could heighten tensions and disrupt critical global oil shipments through the region.

events.title: Fire on Bulker in Gdansk Port
events.description: A large fire broke out on a bulker loading scrap metal at the Port of Gdansk, Poland, prompting a significant firefighting response.

events.title: MSC revises Britannia service rotation
events.description: MSC has revised the rotation of its Britannia service, removing calls at Zeebrugge, Mundra, and the westbound call at Colombo. The updated route now includes Felixstowe, Rotterdam, Antwerp, Gdansk, Gdynia, Klaipeda, Bremerhaven, and London.

events.title: China urges unimpeded Hormuz passage amid fee discussions
events.description: China has called for the unhindered flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as reports emerge that European powers may accept payment demands from Iran and Oman for vessel passage.

events.title: Japan Considering Iranian Oil Resumption Amid Shipping Risks
events.description: Japan is considering resuming Iranian oil purchases under a U.S. sanctions waiver, but buyers are seeking extended waivers and assurances about ship safety amid ongoing shipping risks.

events.title: DP World upgrades Port of Tartous with new cranes
events.description: DP World has delivered the first of three new Mobile Harbour Cranes to the Port of Tartous, Syria, as part of an $800 million investment to modernise the port and increase cargo-handling capacity by 40%.

events.title: German rail freight declines in 2025
events.description: Germany's rail freight sector declined by 5.4% in 2025 due to weaker industrial activity and DB Cargo restructuring, according to the Federal Network Agency.

events.title: Port of Maputo implements first Port Community System
events.description: The Port of Maputo has selected Kalé Logistics Solutions to develop Mozambique's first Port Community System, aiming to enhance digital transformation and trade efficiency in the region.

events.title: New container terminal development at Lach Huyen Port
events.location: Lach Huyen Port, Hai Phong, Vietnam
events.description: PSA Vietnam and Lach Huyen International Logistics & Industrial Park (LHF) have signed an agreement to jointly develop and operate four deep-sea container berths at Lach Huyen Port in Hai Phong, northern Vietnam.

events.title: Increased cargo theft risk during US holiday
events.description: Reduced staffing and freight activity over the US Independence Day holiday are creating opportunities for organized crime to target cargo, according to Verisk CargoNet. Theft activity historically peaks on July 3.

events.title: New vessels deployed to Asia-Latin America routes
events.description: Shipping lines are deploying newly delivered vessels, including the methanol-enabled CMA CGM Copper, to the Asia-Latin America trade lane. This reflects increased capacity and focus on the Asia-West Coast South America route.

events.title: India-LatAm freight space shortage disrupts exports
events.description: Indian exporters to Latin America face major supply chain disruptions due to reduced container space as carriers reallocate capacity for the early Asia peak season, causing delays and rising freight rates.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd acquires stake in Hamburg terminal CTH
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd's subsidiary Hanseatic Global Terminals is acquiring a 20% stake in Eurogate's Hamburg terminal CTH, potentially signaling a strategic industry move that could lead to a future takeover of Eurogate.

events.title: Container carriers raise rates on key east-west routes
events.description: Container carriers have introduced rate hikes on major east-west trades, causing a resurgence in spot freight rates during peak season. The increases follow a previous round of price announcements that led to significant week-on-week rate growth.
